author: barnabasbusa
content: testing matrix I'm using now:

author: barnabasbusa
content:
```yaml
participants_matrix:
  el:
    - el_type: ethrex
      el_image: ethpandaops/ethrex:glamsterdam-devnet-5
    - el_type: nethermind
      el_image: ethpandaops/nethermind:glamsterdam-devnet-5
  cl:
    - cl_type: lodestar
      cl_image: ethpandaops/lodestar:glamsterdam-devnet-5
    - cl_type: lighthouse
      cl_image: ethpandaops/lighthouse:glamsterdam-devnet-5
    - cl_type: grandine
      cl_image: ethpandaops/grandine:glamsterdam-devnet-5-minimal
    - cl_type: nimbus
      cl_image: ethpandaops/nimbus-eth2:glamsterdam-devnet-5-minimal
network_params:
  preset: minimal
  gloas_fork_epoch: 1
  withdrawal_type: "0x01"
  validator_balance: 40000
  genesis_gaslimit: 150000000
  gas_limit: 150000000

additional_services:
   - assertoor
   - spamoor
   - dora

assertoor_params:
  run_stability_check: false
  run_block_proposal_check: false
  tests:
    - { file: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ethpandaops/assertoor/refs/heads/master/playbooks/gloas-dev/builder-lifecycle.yaml" }


port_publisher:
  additional_services:
    enabled: true




```

author: 0xunclebill
content: I think lodestar isn’t serving columns for epoch 0 once we fork to Gloas. Lighthouse custody backfill syncs and peers at epoch 0  aren’t serving columns as expected.

author: 0xunclebill
content: That’s the only peer scoring issue, I disabled peer scoring for custody column backfill in the previous devnet. Can do it again, but there’s a bug somewhere and I’m pretty sure it’s not us

author: barnabasbusa
content: you should be able to get the columns from others?

author: barnabasbusa
content: if it’s a lodestar only bug others would score them down too no?

author: 0xunclebill
content: Nobody else custody backfills

author: 0xunclebill
content: Our cgc changes after genesis

author: 0xunclebill
content: so we go back and backfill missing columns from epoch 0

author: 0xunclebill
content:
LH disabled mplex by default recently, was part of the peering issue, need to pass in `--enable-mplex` or we wont peer with certain clients (i think nimbus?)

```
participants_matrix:
  el:
    - el_type: ethrex
      el_image: ethpandaops/ethrex:glamsterdam-devnet-5
    - el_type: nethermind
      el_image: ethpandaops/nethermind:glamsterdam-devnet-5
  cl:
    - cl_type: lodestar
      cl_image: ethpandaops/lodestar:glamsterdam-devnet-5
    - cl_type: lighthouse
      cl_image: ethpandaops/lighthouse:glamsterdam-devnet-5
      cl_extra_params: ["--enable-mplex"]   
    - cl_type: grandine
      cl_image: ethpandaops/grandine:glamsterdam-devnet-5-minimal
    - cl_type: nimbus
      cl_image: ethpandaops/nimbus-eth2:glamsterdam-devnet-5-minimal
network_params:
  preset: minimal
  gloas_fork_epoch: 1
  withdrawal_type: "0x01"
  validator_balance: 40000
  genesis_gaslimit: 150000000
  gas_limit: 150000000

additional_services:
   - assertoor
   - spamoor
   - dora

global_log_level: debug

assertoor_params:
  run_stability_check: false
  run_block_proposal_check: false
  tests:
    - { file: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ethpandaops/assertoor/refs/heads/master/playbooks/gloas-dev/builder-lifecycle.yaml" }


port_publisher:
  additional_services:
    enabled: true
```
